description: After lifting stones, tending a fever, or thinking past the point of usefulness, someone may 'therilu'. The verb names a pause or reduction in activity that allows capacity to recover. Rest may be chosen or required, and it does not promise that recovery will follow.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip lightly between or against the upper teeth and let unvoiced air pass for 'th', then move into middle-height front 'e'. Make a tap or short trill at the alveolar ridge before stressed high-front 'i'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l', let air pass along its sides, and finish with rounded high-back 'u'.
sound_symbolism: Air slips past the teeth before the brief central tap, then lateral 'lu' releases the word into a rounded finish. The consonants keep moving, but none asks the mouth for much force.
usage_notes: Choose 'therilu' when recovery or renewed capacity is the point of the pause. Cessative 'te' can end any activity without implying rest, 'nulae' names sleep, and module word 'hasenu' names a bounded recess expected to end with resumption.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Plans for work and shared infrastructure can include the limits of bodies, tools, and ecosystems. 'therilu' makes recovery time speakable without treating it as failed production.

semantic_domains.activity: pausing or reducing activity for recovery
semantic_domains.physical: rest of a body or bodily capacity
semantic_domains.temporal: an interval between periods of greater activity
